name: Use Searchable Names
source: Clean Code by Robert C. Martin, Chapter 2: Meaningful Names
problem: Single-letter variables and bare numeric literals cannot be located reliably with a text search, making it hard to find all usages, understand purpose, or safely change them.
steps: Replace bare numeric literals with named constants that describe their meaning.
Avoid single-letter variable names except for loop counters inside very short, tightly scoped loops.
Name constants in UPPER_SNAKE_CASE so they are visually distinct and searchable.
Keep the length of a name proportional to the size of its scope.
Validate that each identifier can be found unambiguously with a simple text search.
